 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-cwcj","title":"Add share_links schema tables","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-318.

## Task
Add share_links table for private link sharing of content.

## Schema Definition
```typescript
export const shareLinks = pgTable("share_links", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  userId: uuid("user_id").notNull().references(() => users.id),
  
  // What is being shared
  contentType: text("content_type", { 
    enum: ["user_prompt", "prompt_pack", "skill", "collection"] 
  }).notNull(),
  contentId: uuid("content_id").notNull(),
  
  // The shareable link code (appears in URL)
  linkCode: text("link_code").notNull().unique(),
  
  // Optional security
  passwordHash: text("password_hash"),  // bcrypt hash if password protected
  
  // Expiration
  expiresAt: timestamp("expires_at", { withTimezone: true }),
  
  // Stats
  viewCount: integer("view_count").default(0),
  
  // Status
  isActive: boolean("is_active").default(true).notNull(),
  
  // Timestamps
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow().notNull(),
}, (table) => [
  index("idx_share_links_user").on(table.userId),
  index("idx_share_links_code").on(table.linkCode),
  index("idx_share_links_content").on(table.contentType, table.contentId),
]);

export const shareLinkViews = pgTable("share_link_views", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  linkId: uuid("link_id").notNull().references(() => shareLinks.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
  
  // Viewer info (optional - null for anonymous)
  viewerId: uuid("viewer_id").references(() => users.id),
  
  // For analytics without PII
  ipHash: text("ip_hash"),  // SHA-256 of IP for unique visitor counting
  userAgent: text("user_agent"),
  
  viewedAt: timestamp("viewed_at", { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow().notNull(),
}, (table) => [
  index("idx_share_link_views_link").on(table.linkId),
]);
```

## Link Code Generation
- 12 character alphanumeric (base62)
- Example: "x7KmN2pQ4rYz"
- Not sequential/guessable
- Check for collisions on insert

## Public URLs
- /share/{linkCode} - view shared content
- No auth required to view
- Optional password prompt

## Notes
- viewCount is denormalized; increment on each view
- isActive allows "revoking" without deleting
- ipHash allows counting unique visitors without storing IPs

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-d4t1","title":"Implement recently viewed history","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-7wcm.

## Task
Track and display user's recently viewed prompts/content for easy access.

## Why This Matters
- Quick access to recently browsed content
- Find that prompt you saw earlier
- Reduce re-search friction
- Personalization signal

## Features

### 1. Tracking
- Track prompt views
- Track collection views
- Track skill views
- Track search queries

### 2. History Display
- Recent items in sidebar
- Full history page
- Searchable history
- Clear history option

### 3. Privacy
- Only track for logged-in users
- Clear on request
- Auto-expire after 30 days
- Not shared with others

## Database Schema
```typescript
export const viewHistory = pgTable('view_history', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  
  // What was viewed
  resourceType: text('resource_type').notNull(), // prompt, collection, skill, pack
  resourceId: uuid('resource_id').notNull(),
  
  // Context
  source: text('source'), // search, browse, direct, recommendation
  searchQuery: text('search_query'), // if from search
  
  // Timestamps
  viewedAt: timestamp('viewed_at').defaultNow(),
  duration: integer('duration'), // seconds on page (optional)
}, (table) => [
  index('idx_history_user').on(table.userId),
  index('idx_history_resource').on(table.resourceType, table.resourceId),
  index('idx_history_time').on(table.viewedAt),
]);
```

## Implementation

### Track View
```typescript
async function trackView(userId: string, resource: ViewResource) {
  // Dedupe rapid views (ignore if viewed in last 5 min)
  const recent = await getRecentView(userId, resource.id);
  if (recent && isRecent(recent.viewedAt, 5 * 60 * 1000)) {
    return; // Skip duplicate
  }
  
  await db.insert(viewHistory).values({
    userId,
    resourceType: resource.type,
    resourceId: resource.id,
    source: resource.source,
  });
}
```

### Get History
```typescript
async function getRecentlyViewed(userId: string, limit = 10) {
  return db.select()
    .from(viewHistory)
    .where(eq(viewHistory.userId, userId))
    .orderBy(desc(viewHistory.viewedAt))
    .limit(limit);
}
```

## UI Components
- RecentlyViewedSidebar - Quick access list
- HistoryPage - Full browsable history
- ClearHistoryButton - Privacy control

## Files to Create
- src/lib/db/schema/history.ts
- src/lib/history/tracking.ts
- src/app/api/history/route.ts
- src/app/(app)/history/page.tsx
- src/components/history/recently-viewed.tsx

## Success Criteria
- Views tracked accurately
- Deduplication works
- History accessible
- Privacy controls work

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-dc1k","title":"Implement comments and discussions on prompts","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-4aeq.

## Task
Allow users to comment on and discuss public prompts.

## Why This Matters
- Community engagement
- Feedback for creators
- Knowledge sharing
- Questions and answers

## Features

### 1. Comment System
- Add comments to public prompts
- Reply to comments (threaded)
- Edit own comments
- Delete own comments
- Report inappropriate comments

### 2. Discussion Features
- Upvote helpful comments
- Pin important comments (creator only)
- Sort by newest/oldest/popular
- Collapse/expand threads
- Load more pagination

### 3. Notifications
- Notify creator of new comments
- Notify on replies to your comments
- Notify on mentions (@username)
- Email digest option

### 4. Moderation
- Creator can hide comments
- Creator can disable comments
- Admin can remove comments
- Spam detection

## Database Schema
```typescript
export const promptComments = pgTable('prompt_comments', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  promptId: uuid('prompt_id').references(() => userPrompts.id).notNull(),
  authorId: uuid('author_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  
  // Content
  content: text('content').notNull(),
  
  // Threading
  parentId: uuid('parent_id').references(() => promptComments.id),
  depth: integer('depth').default(0),
  
  // Status
  isHidden: boolean('is_hidden').default(false),
  isPinned: boolean('is_pinned').default(false),
  
  // Engagement
  upvoteCount: integer('upvote_count').default(0),
  replyCount: integer('reply_count').default(0),
  
  // Timestamps
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
  editedAt: timestamp('edited_at'),
}, (table) => [
  index('idx_comments_prompt').on(table.promptId),
  index('idx_comments_author').on(table.authorId),
  index('idx_comments_parent').on(table.parentId),
]);

export const commentUpvotes = pgTable('comment_upvotes', {
  commentId: uuid('comment_id').references(() => promptComments.id).notNull(),
  userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
}, (table) => [
  primaryKey({ columns: [table.commentId, table.userId] }),
]);
```

## API Endpoints
- GET /api/prompts/[id]/comments
- POST /api/prompts/[id]/comments
- PATCH /api/comments/[id]
- DELETE /api/comments/[id]
- POST /api/comments/[id]/upvote
- POST /api/comments/[id]/report

## UI Components
- CommentSection - Full comment display
- CommentThread - Single thread
- CommentForm - New comment input
- CommentItem - Individual comment
- ReplyButton, UpvoteButton

## Limits
- Max 5000 characters per comment
- Max 10 levels of nesting
- Rate limit: 10 comments/hour
- Premium: higher limits

## Files to Create
- src/lib/db/schema/comments.ts
- src/app/api/prompts/[id]/comments/route.ts
- src/app/api/comments/[id]/route.ts
- src/components/comments/
- src/lib/comments/moderation.ts

## Success Criteria
- Comments work smoothly
- Threading displays correctly
- Notifications sent
- Moderation tools work

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-del9","title":"E2E Tests: CLI Authentication Flow","description":"Create comprehensive E2E tests for CLI authentication covering both local browser and remote device code flows.

Test Scenarios:
- Local browser flow: happy path, port conflicts, timeout, browser launch failure
- Remote device code flow: happy path, code expiration, slow poll, denied, invalid code
- Token persistence: save/load credentials, auto-refresh, re-login on expiry
- Integration tests: full login->whoami->list->logout flow

Logging Requirements:
- Log each test step with timestamps
- Log HTTP requests/responses (sanitize tokens)
- Log credential file operations
- Log browser/process spawning events
- Dump full state on failure for debugging

Test Infrastructure:
- Use Bun test runner
- Mock HTTP server for premium site endpoints
- Temporary credential directory per test
- Cleanup hooks for all resources

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-uw7z","title":"CLI: Add premium login command for authenticated access","description":"## Task
Add a login command to the jfp CLI to authenticate with JeffreysPrompts Premium.

## Context
The jfp CLI currently accesses only the static free-tier prompts. Premium users should be able to:
- Authenticate their CLI to access their personal library
- Sync saved prompts to local machine
- Access premium-only content

## Implementation
Use the device code flow (RFC 8628) that the premium project implements:
1. jfp login → opens browser to premium verification page
2. User confirms in browser
3. CLI receives token and stores securely
4. Subsequent commands include auth token

## Integration Points
- Premium API: /api/cli/device-code (from premium project)
- Premium API: /api/cli/token (from premium project)
- Local storage: ~/.jeffreysprompts/credentials.json

## Commands
- jfp login - Start device code flow
- jfp logout - Clear stored credentials
- jfp whoami - Show current authenticated user
- jfp status - Show auth status and subscription tier

## Success Criteria
- CLI can authenticate to premium API
- Credentials stored securely
- Token refresh handled automatically
- Clear error messages for auth failures

## Cross-Repo Integration
- Premium device code API endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-0o1
- Premium device code verification page: jeffreysprompts_premium-qdm
- Premium CLI auth page (local browser flow): jeffreysprompts_premium-mn0
- Premium CLI JWT/token generation: jeffreysprompts_premium-qnl
- Premium token refresh/revoke endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-0ve
- Premium device_codes schema table: jeffreysprompts_premium-h8r
- Premium CLI device code integration tests: jeffreysprompts_premium-wdao","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T05:30:26.577649305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T08:20:49.142382389Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T08:20:49.142382389Z","close_reason":"Added auth status/tier info to jfp status output","compaction_level":0}

## Task
Create E2E tests for the recently viewed history feature.

## Test Scenarios
1. **History Recording**
   - Viewing prompt adds to history
   - Viewing collection adds to history
   - Viewing profile adds to history
   - Duplicate views update timestamp

2. **History Display**
   - Recent items show in sidebar/dropdown
   - Correct order (most recent first)
   - Item type indicators
   - Click navigates correctly

3. **History Management**
   - Clear all history
   - Remove single item
   - History respects max limit

4. **Privacy**
   - History is private
   - Not visible to others
   - Clear on logout option

## Logging Requirements
- Log history additions
- Log history access
- Log clear actions
- Screenshot history UI

## Files to Create
- e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts
- e2e/lib/history-helpers.ts

## Success Criteria
- History accurately recorded
- UI displays correctly
- Clear functions work
- Privacy maintained

","notes":"## Completion Notes - CoralStar

### Files Created
- `e2e/lib/history-helpers.ts` - Helper functions for history tests
- `e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts` - Main test file with 19 test cases

### Files Modified
- `e2e/lib/index.ts` - Added history-helpers export
- `e2e/playwright.config.ts` - Added history/**/*.spec.ts to testMatch

### Test Coverage (19 tests × 2 browsers = 38 total)

**History Recording (5 tests)**
- records prompt view in history
- records collection view in history
- records search query in history
- duplicate views within 5 minutes return existing entry
- different resource types create separate entries

**History Retrieval (4 tests)**
- returns history in most recent first order
- respects limit parameter
- filters by resource type
- returns empty array for user with no history

**History Management (2 tests)**
- clears all history for user
- respects maximum limit of 100 entries

**Privacy (2 tests)**
- history is isolated between users
- clearing one users history does not affect another

**Validation (6 tests)**
- rejects requests without userId
- rejects invalid resource type
- requires resourceId for non-search types
- requires searchQuery for search type
- rejects excessively long userId
- rejects invalid JSON body

### Notes
- Tests use API-only approach since UI components for history display are not yet implemented
- Helper functions include selectors for future UI tests
- Playwright test listing confirms all 38 tests are recognized","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralStar","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:23:30.318372011Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:16:04.566932225Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-v2r0","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-d4t1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:25:35.545441905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-v6re","title":"Set up Sentry error tracking and monitoring","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-2rk. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Set up Sentry for error tracking and performance monitoring.

## Why This Matters
From AGENTS.md Deployment section: "Sentry for error reporting (optional)"
- Catch errors in production before users report them
- Track error frequency and impact
- Monitor performance regressions
- Debug issues with full context

## Implementation

### 1. Sentry Configuration
```typescript
// sentry.client.config.ts
import * as Sentry from "@sentry/nextjs";

Sentry.init({
  dsn: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN,
  environment: process.env.NODE_ENV,
  tracesSampleRate: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' ? 0.1 : 1.0,
  replaysSessionSampleRate: 0.1,
  replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0,
  integrations: [
    Sentry.replayIntegration(),
  ],
  beforeSend(event) {
    // Don't send events in development
    if (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development') {
      return null;
    }
    return event;
  },
});
```

```typescript
// sentry.server.config.ts
import * as Sentry from "@sentry/nextjs";

Sentry.init({
  dsn: process.env.SENTRY_DSN,
  environment: process.env.NODE_ENV,
  tracesSampleRate: 0.1,
});
```

### 2. Error Boundary
```typescript
// src/components/error-boundary.tsx
"use client";

import * as Sentry from "@sentry/nextjs";

export function ErrorBoundary({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <Sentry.ErrorBoundary
      fallback={({ error, resetError }) => (
        <div className="error-fallback">
          <h2>Something went wrong</h2>
          <button onClick={resetError}>Try again</button>
        </div>
      )}
      onError={(error) => {
        console.error('Caught error:', error);
      }}
    >
      {children}
    </Sentry.ErrorBoundary>
  );
}
```

### 3. Manual Error Capture
```typescript
// In API routes or server actions
try {
  await riskyOperation();
} catch (error) {
  Sentry.captureException(error, {
    tags: { component: 'billing' },
    extra: { userId, action: 'checkout' },
  });
  throw error;
}
```

### 4. User Context
```typescript
// After authentication
Sentry.setUser({
  id: user.id,
  email: user.email,
  subscription: user.tier,
});

// On sign out
Sentry.setUser(null);
```

### 5. Performance Monitoring
```typescript
// Custom spans for expensive operations
const transaction = Sentry.startTransaction({
  name: 'Search Query',
  op: 'search',
});

const span = transaction.startChild({
  op: 'db.query',
  description: 'Full-text search',
});

// ... perform operation ...

span.finish();
transaction.finish();
```

## Alert Configuration
- Alert on new error types
- Alert on error rate spike (>10/min)
- Alert on performance regression (>2s p95)
- Weekly digest of top errors

## Source Maps
- Upload source maps during build
- Associate with release version
- Works with Vercel deployment

## Environment Variables
```env
NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN=https://...@sentry.io/...
SENTRY_DSN=https://...@sentry.io/...
SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN=... (for source maps)
SENTRY_ORG=jeffreysprompts
SENTRY_PROJECT=premium
```

## Files to Create
- `sentry.client.config.ts`
- `sentry.server.config.ts`
- `sentry.edge.config.ts`
- `src/components/error-boundary.tsx`
- `next.config.ts` (add Sentry plugin)

## Dependencies
```json
{
  "@sentry/nextjs": "^8.0.0"
}
```

## Success Criteria
- Errors are captured in production
- Source maps enable readable stack traces
- User context is attached to errors
- Performance is monitored
- Alerts are configured
